old_value: {"id":"sxAct-zaj","title":"Implement symbol registry and ValidateSymbol equivalent in Julia","description":"Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore symbol registry and ValidateSymbol. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m lines 678-779. Responsibilities: (1) a module-level registry mapping symbol names to owning packages, (2) validate_symbol(name) that throws if the name collides with reserved names or is already registered by another package, (3) register_symbol(name, package) that adds to the registry, (4) Julia equivalents of the dollar-Names lists ($xCoreNames, $xTensorNames, etc.). Unit tests must cover all error branches from the original ValidateSymbol implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T16:48:06Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:43:16Z"}

new_value: Central _symbol_registry Dict + register_symbol + 7 new per-package name lists + updated ValidateSymbol. Tests in test/runtests.jl: 31 pass, 1 skip.

old_value: {"id":"sxAct-3du","title":"Implement extension mechanism (xTension/MakexTensions) in Julia","description":"Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore extension mechanism (xTension and MakexTensions). Background: xTension lets packages register hooks that fire at the Beginning or End of def-commands like DefMetric, without modifying the original package source. In Julia this should be a hook registry: xtension(package, defcommand, moment, fn) registers fn, and make_xtensions(defcommand, moment, args...) fires all registered hooks for that command-moment combination. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m line 606. Unit tests must verify hooks fire in registration order and at both Beginning and End moments.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T16:48:08Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:43:21Z"}

new_value: xTension!/MakexTensions already implemented in section 8. Added 6 test sets covering: registration order, Beginning/End independence, args passing, no-op with no hooks, invalid moment error, multi-command independence.

old_value: {"id":"sxAct-2nf","title":"Implement programming utilities in Julia (list ops, options, linking, dagger)","description":"Implement Julia equivalents of xCore programming utility functions that are either thin wrappers or have no direct stdlib replacement. Based on must-implement and stdlib-equivalent categories from sxAct-0q8. Covers: (1) list utilities: just_one, map_if_plus, thread_array, (2) option utilities: check_options, true_or_false, report_set, report_set_option, (3) symbol linking: symbol_join, link_symbols, unlink_symbol, (4) dagger utilities: has_dagger_q, make_dagger_symbol. Where Julia stdlib is a direct replacement, document the mapping in a comment and do not implement a wrapper. Unit tests for all non-trivial implementations.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T16:48:13Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:43:32Z"}

new_value: Added ThreadArray, LinkCharacter/LinkSymbols/UnlinkSymbol, ReportSet, ReportSetOption. Existing JustOne/MapIfPlus/CheckOptions/TrueOrFalse/SymbolJoin/HasDaggerCharacterQ/MakeDaggerSymbol were already complete. 13 new tests.

old_value: {"id":"sxAct-3cv","title":"Write Layer 1 unit tests and Layer 2 property tests for Julia xCore","description":"Write Layer 1 unit tests and Layer 2 property tests for the Julia xCore implementation. L1 tests: deterministic tests in tests/unit/test_xcore_julia.py or src/julia/test/runtests.jl, targeting over 90 percent coverage of the public Julia XCore API. L2 tests: property-based tests for symbol registry invariants (no two packages register the same name, validate_symbol always throws on collisions, hook registration ordering preserved). TOML-format xCore tests from sxAct-4kw run via the TOML runner from sxAct-z8g. All L1 tests must pass without the oracle running.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T16:48:19Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:43:49Z"}

new_value: 93 test sets (L1 unit + L2 property) added to src/julia/test/runtests.jl, all passing. Also fixed CheckOptions Pair-as-atomic bug.

old_value: {"id":"sxAct-caj","title":"Create Python wrapper for Julia xCore","description":"Create the Python wrapper for Julia XCore.jl using PythonCall.jl (preferred) or PyJulia. Expose all public Julia xCore functions under the sxact.xcore Python namespace. The wrapper must: (1) initialize Julia runtime once per process, (2) surface Julia exceptions as Python exceptions with clear messages, (3) handle type conversions across the language boundary. Performance overhead must not exceed 2x of direct Julia execution per sxAct-k0a acceptance criteria. Output: src/sxact/xcore/ Python package. Smoke test: from sxact.xcore import validate_symbol and calling it succeeds.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T16:48:15Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:43:37Z"}

new_value: Python wrapper implemented in src/sxact/xcore/ using juliacall/PythonCall.jl. All functions exposed with snake_case names, type conversions handled, performance 1.23x (well within 2x limit). Smoke test passes.

old_value: {"id":"sxAct-8w4","title":"Write tests for Python xCore wrapper (sxact.xcore)","description":"Write tests for the Python xCore wrapper (sxAct-caj) at src/sxact/xcore/. Tests must cover: (1) all public Python API functions call through to Julia correctly, (2) Julia exceptions surface as Python exceptions with readable messages, (3) type conversions are correct across the language boundary (strings, numbers, lists), (4) performance overhead is measured and confirmed within 2x of direct Julia execution per sxAct-k0a acceptance criteria. Test file: tests/unit/test_xcore_python.py. Must not duplicate L1 Julia tests - focus on the Python boundary.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T17:40:59Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-02-23T17:45:10Z"}
